This README has two major entries: The first one is for Thumbnail as an independent component, with all its variants, and the second one is for ThumbnailMultiple, a container that uses Thumbnail as children in a grid or stacked layout. This index might be useful: 

- [`Thumbnail`](#@andes/thumbnail)
- [`ThumbnailMultiple`](#ThumbnailMultiple)

# @andes/thumbnail

Thumbnail Component, can be used with an image, an icon, text or a custom node.

## How to use

### Installation

> Follow the [general installation guide](../../docs/installation.md).

```bash

$ npm install --save @andes/thumbnail

```

### Styles

> Follow the [general styles usage guide](../../docs/styles-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your bundle:

```css

@import '@andes/thumbnail/index';

```

### Scripts

> Follow the [general scripts usage guide](../../docs/scripts-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your page:

```jsx

import { Thumbnail } from '@andes/thumbnail';

```

## Examples

For full demo and configuration options, check out the [story for Thumbnail](https://andes.adminml.com/web/?path=/story/thumbnail--andes-thumbnail).

```jsx
<Thumbnail>
  <img alt="avatar" src="https://http2.mlstatic.com/path/to/some/image.webp" />
</Thumbnail>
```

With frontend-image

```jsx
import Image from 'frontend-image';

<Thumbnail>
  <Image alt="avatar" src="https://http2.mlstatic.com/path/to/some/image.webp" />
</Thumbnail>
```

With Text

```jsx
<Thumbnail srLabel="John Doe">
  <p>JD</p>
</Thumbnail>
```

With Badge support

```jsx
import { Dot } from '@andes/badge';

<Thumbnail
  badge={<Dot />}
>
  <img alt="avatar" src="https://http2.mlstatic.com/path/to/some/image.webp" />
</Thumbnail>
```

With Icon support

```jsx
import Camera24 from '@andes/icons/Camera24';

<Thumbnail
  hierarchy='loud'
>
  <Camera24 />
</Thumbnail>
```

## Props

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `badge` | Badge support. This can be Dot or Pill | `element` | `undefined` |  |
| `children` | Custom content | `element` |  | ✅ |
| `className` | Custom class | `string` | `undefined` | |
| `color` | Custom color definition | `Color` | `undefined` | |
| `disabled` | Whether the thumbnail should be disabled, it is only supported on the icon content. | `bool` | `false` |  |
| `hierarchy` | Styles of the Thumbnail, it is only supported on the icon content. | `string: 'loud', 'mute', 'quiet'` | `mute` |  |
| `id` | Unique identifier | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `modifier` | Type of the thumbnail, it can be `circle` or `square` | `string` | `'circle'` |  |
| `size` | Thumbnail size. Available values are:  `'16'`,`'24'`, `'32'`, `'40'`, `'48'`, `'56'`, `'64'`, `'72'`, `'80'` | `string` | `'48'` |  |
| `srLabel` | A description needed in all types of thumbnails for screen readers and for displaying an alternative text when you hover over thumbanil | `string` | `undefined` |  |

## Accessibility

1- Be careful. Custom nodes as `children` may need a role and aria-abel prop to improve screen reader user's experience ([recommended link](https://developer.mozilla.org/es/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/ARIA_Techniques)).

2- Text Thumbnail is intended to be used for initials like the seller's or the buyer's name.

3- Badge component has its own support of the srLabel property.

# ThumbnailMultiple

Thumbnail Multiple is a container meant to be used with @andes/thumbnail to display various thumbnail.

There are two types of Thumbnail Multiple:

- Grid: Thumbnails are displayed in a grid layout.
- Stacked: Thumbnails are displayed in a single line, overlapping with the previous one.

The UI specs are defined in _[Andes UI](http://andes.adminml.com) > [ThumbnailMultiple](https://andes.mercadolibre.com/924dc6ec5/p/55eb4c-thumbnail/b/26f535)_.

## How to use

### Installation

> Follow the [general installation guide](../../docs/installation.md).

```bash

$ npm install --save @andes/thumbnail

```

### Styles

> Follow the [general styles usage guide](../../docs/styles-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component with all the styles in your bundle:

```css

@import '@andes/thumbnail/index';

```

Or you can import only the styles that you need in your project like this:

```css
/* Base */
@import '@andes/thumbnail/base';

/* For type: grid */
@import '@andes/thumbnail/lib/styles/multiple-grid';
/* For tipe: stacked */
@import '@andes/thumbnail/lib/styles/multiple-stacked';
```

### Scripts

> Follow the [general scripts usage guide](../../docs/scripts-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your page:

```jsx

import { Thumbnail, ThumbnailMultiple } from '@andes/thumbnail';

```

## Examples

For full demo and configuration options, check out the [story for Thumbnailmultiple](https://andes.adminml.com/web/?selectedKind=thumbnailmultiple).

```jsx
  <ThumbnailMultiple 
    modifier="circle" 
    containerSize="56"
    type="grid" 
    overflow={3}>
    <Thumbnail>
      <Image
        alt="avatar"
        src="https://http2.mlstatic.com/remeras-personalizadas-geek-retro-vintage-pop-games-D_NQ_NP_16326-MLA20118276631_062014-F.webp"
      />
    </Thumbnail>
    <Thumbnail>
      <Image
        alt="avatar"
        src="https://http2.mlstatic.com/remeras-personalizadas-geek-retro-vintage-pop-games-D_NQ_NP_16326-MLA20118276631_062014-F.webp"
      />
    </Thumbnail>
    <Thumbnail>
      <Image
        alt="avatar"
        src="https://http2.mlstatic.com/remeras-personalizadas-geek-retro-vintage-pop-games-D_NQ_NP_16326-MLA20118276631_062014-F.webp"
      />
    </Thumbnail>
  </ThumbnailMultiple>
```

## Props

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `children` | Custom content, should be a collection of `<Thumbnail>` | `element` |  | ✅ |
| `className` | Custom class. | string | `''` | |
| `containerSize` | Container size. For type `grid`, the accepted sizes are: `'40'`, `'48'`, `'56'`, `'64'`, `'72'`, `'80'`. For type `stacked`, also the sizes `'24'`, `'32'` are accepted | `string` | `'56'` |  |
| `id` | Unique identifier | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `modifier` | Type of the thumbnail, it can be `circle` or `square` | `string` | `'circle'` |  |
| `overflow` | Indicates the number of elements that overflow | `number` | `undefined` |  |
| `type` | Type of the container layout, could be `grid` or `stacked` | `string` | `'grid'` |  |

## Theming

This component supports theming. It allows you to pass a `customTheme` object to it adjusting colors, typography, spacing and styles to align with a brand's visual identity, user preferences or project requirements.

<br />

The `customTheme` object can make use of values ​​referenced in the component or CSS properties for quick customization or make use of style overrides according to `data-attributes` specific to the component for more complex cases. We do not recommend the latter since it will be deprecated in the future.

<br />

### Using Referenced Component Values

The Thumbnail allows you to modify the following attributes depending on its reference value.

| Sub Attribute | Description               |
| ------------- | ------------------------- |
| `fill`        | Thumbnail fill color.     |
| `text`        | Thumbnail text color.     |
| `icon`        | Thumbnail icon fill color.|
| `border`      | Thumbnail border color.   |

```jsx
const customTheme = {
  components: {
    thumbnail: {
      [key]: {
        color: {
          border: {
            default: borderColor,
          },
        },
      },
      [hierarchy]: {
        color: {
          fill: {
            default: thumbnailMuteFillColor,
          },
          text: {
            default: thumbnailMuteTextColor,
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};
```

Possible `key` values (see [theme json files](../themes/meli-themes/) for structure):

```

image | loud | quiet | mute | color | badge | grid | stacked

```

Possible `hierarchy` values:

```

hierarchy: loud | quiet | mute

```

> More states may be added in the future, so we recommend consulting the @andes/themes/meli-themes/ml.json file for more detailed information

### Example Thumbnail Default

<img src="resources/thumbnail/docs/Thumbnail-Example-Reference-Theming-Default.png" alt="Reference Theming Thumbnail Example of Thumbnail of type Image with red border." width="300" />

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Thumbnail } from '@andes/thumbnail';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import { PillIcon } from '@andes/badge';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    thumbnail: {
      color: {
        border: {
          default: 'red',
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <Thumbnail badge={<PillIcon />}>
      <img
        alt="avatar"
        src="https://http2.mlstatic.com/remeras-personalizadas-geek-retro-vintage-pop-games-D_NQ_NP_16326-MLA20118276631_062014-F.webp"
      />
    </Thumbnail>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/thumbnail/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Example Thumbnail Text

<img src="resources/thumbnail/docs/Thumbnail-Example-Reference-Theming-Text.png" alt="Reference Theming Thumbnail Example of Thumbnail of type Text with fill color orange, border and text color turquoise." width="300" />


```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Thumbnail } from '@andes/thumbnail';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    thumbnail: {
      color: {
        border: {
          default: 'turquoise',
        },
      },
      mute: {
        color: {
          fill: {
            default: 'orange',
          },
          text: {
            default: 'turquoise',
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <Thumbnail>
      <span>JD</span>
    </Thumbnail>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/thumbnail/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Example Thumbnail Icon

<img src="resources/thumbnail/docs/Thumbnail-Example-Reference-Theming-Icon.png" alt="Reference Theming Thumbnail Example of Thumbnail of type Icon, with border and fill color green." width="300" />


```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Thumbnail } from '@andes/thumbnail';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import Camera24 from '@andes/icons/Camera24';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    thumbnail: {
      color: {
        border: {
          default: 'green',
        },
      },
      mute: {
        color: {
          icon: {
            default: 'green',
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <Thumbnail>
      <Camera24 />
    </Thumbnail>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/thumbnail/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Example Thumbnail Multiple Grid

<img src="resources/thumbnail/docs/Thumbnail-Example-Reference-Theming-Multiple-Grid.png" alt="Reference Theming Thumbnail Example of Thumbnail of type Multiple Grid, with overflow text color magenta." width="300" />


```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Thumbnail, ThumbnailMultiple } from '@andes/thumbnail';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    thumbnail: {
      grid: {
        overflow: {
          color: {
            text: 'magenta',
          },
        },
      },
      stacked: {
        overflow: {
          color: {
            text: 'magenta',
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <ThumbnailMultiple
      containerSize="56"
      modifier="circle"
      overflow={3}
      type="grid"
    >
      <Thumbnail>
        <img
          alt="avatar"
          src="https://http2.mlstatic.com/remeras-personalizadas-geek-retro-vintage-pop-games-D_NQ_NP_16326-MLA20118276631_062014-F.webp"
        />
      </Thumbnail>
      <Thumbnail>
        <img
          alt="avatar"
          src="https://http2.mlstatic.com/remeras-personalizadas-geek-retro-vintage-pop-games-D_NQ_NP_16326-MLA20118276631_062014-F.webp"
        />
      </Thumbnail>
      <Thumbnail>
        <img
          alt="avatar"
          src="https://http2.mlstatic.com/remeras-personalizadas-geek-retro-vintage-pop-games-D_NQ_NP_16326-MLA20118276631_062014-F.webp"
        />
      </Thumbnail>
    </ThumbnailMultiple>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.

### Using `styleOverrides`

This component has a series of `data-attributes` that serve as a hook to style complex cases:

| data-attribute                   | Description                                                                                  |
| ---------------------------------| -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `data-andes-thumbnail`           | Thumbnail main container.                                                                    |
| `data-andes-thumbnail-hierarchy` | Thumbnail Hierarchy. Can be: `'default'`, `'loud'`, `'quiet'`, `'mute'`                      |
| `data-andes-thumbnail-size`      | Thumbnail Size. Can be `'16'`, `'24'`, `'32'`, `'40'`, `'48'`, `'56'`, `'64'`, `'72'`, `'80'`|
| `data-andes-thumbnail-content`   | Thumbnail content.                                                                           |

#### ThumbnailMultiple

| data-attribute                            | Description                                             |
| ----------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| `data-andes-thumbnail-multiple`           | ThumbnailMultiple main container.                       |
| `data-andes-thumbnail-multiple-type`      | ThumbnailMultiple type. Can be `'grid'`, `'stacked'`    |
| `data-andes-thumbnail-multiple-overflow`  | ThumbnailMultiple overflow thumbnail present. `Boolean` |

Taking advantage of the them, you can use an entry called `styleOverrides` where you can add styles according to any `data-attribute`.

### Example

<img src="resources/thumbnail/docs/Thumbnail-Example-Reference-Theming-Data-Attributes.png" alt="Example of Thumnail Text style override using data attributes." width="300" />


```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Thumbnail } from '@andes/thumbnail';
import { AndesThemeProvider } from '@andes/themes';
import './styles.scss';

const customTheme = {
  components: {
    thumbnail: {
      styleOverrides: {
        '&[data-andes-thumbnail-hierarchy="loud"] [data-andes-thumbnail-content]': {
          fontWeight: 'bold',
        },
      }
    },
  },
};

const MyComponent = () => (
  <AndesThemeProvider theme={customTheme}>
    <Thumbnail 
      hierarchy="loud"
    >
      <span>JD</span>
    </Thumbnail>
  </AndesThemeProvider>
);

export default MyComponent;
```

```css
/* styles.scss */
@import '@andes/thumbnail/index';
```

> Remember that it is not necessary for the ThemeProvider to be next to the component you want to customize. Ideally it should be at the App level, wrapping your entire page.
